description: AquaMind AI Response Generator benchmark. Each case supplies a User Query plus a sanitized Updated AggregatedUniversalAgentResponse (status + specialist responses) and an optional RecommendationResponse. The test harness plays the role of the Supervisor Orchestrator: it assembles the deterministic prompt (system prompt, user query, sanitized aggregate) and asserts the FinalResponse is grounded strictly in the supplied evidence.

cases.user_query: What is the groundwater level in Salem?

responses.answer: The latest recorded groundwater level in Salem is 7.8 metres below ground level.
responses.source: Ground Water Year Book of Tamil Nadu 2023-24

cases.user_query: What is groundwater?

responses.answer: Groundwater is water stored beneath the Earth's surface within soil pores and fractures in rock formations.

cases.user_query: Predict the groundwater level in Coimbatore for 2030.

responses.answer: The predicted groundwater level in Coimbatore for 2030 is 11.2 metres below ground level.

cases.user_query: What is the groundwater level in Madurai and what does it mean?

responses.answer: The groundwater level in Madurai is 5.4 metres below ground level.
Depth to water level measures how far below the ground surface the water table lies; shallower levels generally indicate better groundwater availability.

cases.user_query: Predict groundwater in Tiruppur for 2030 and advise what should be done.

responses.answer: The predicted groundwater level in Tiruppur for 2030 is 14.6 metres below ground level, indicating a deepening water table.
A deepening water table increases pumping costs and can reduce long-term aquifer sustainability.
responses.source: Aquifer Management Plan for over-exploited areas in Tiruppur district

recommendations.title: Artificial Recharge
recommendations.description: Construct recharge structures to replenish the aquifer.

recommendations.title: Rainwater Harvesting
recommendations.description: Expand rooftop and community rainwater harvesting to capture monsoon runoff.

cases.user_query: What is the current groundwater level in Erode and what will it be in 2030?

responses.answer: The current groundwater level in Erode is 8.1 metres below ground level.
The predicted groundwater level in Erode for 2030 is 9.7 metres below ground level.

cases.user_query: Give me a full groundwater assessment for Chennai including outlook and advice.

responses.answer: Chennai's current groundwater level is 6.3 metres below ground level and its extraction stage is classified as Over Exploited.
The predicted groundwater level in Chennai for 2030 is 8.9 metres below ground level.
An Over Exploited classification means annual groundwater extraction exceeds the annual recharge, which threatens long-term availability.
responses.source: Dynamic Ground Water Resources of Tamil Nadu 2024

recommendations.title: Artificial Recharge
recommendations.description: Implement recharge shafts and check dams to augment aquifer storage.

recommendations.title: Demand Management
recommendations.description: Promote efficient irrigation and reduce non-essential extraction.

cases.user_query: How is groundwater in Tiruppur district?

responses.answer: Tiruppur district is classified as Over Exploited with a stage of extraction of 142 percent.

recommendations.title: Rainwater Harvesting
recommendations.description: Mandate rainwater harvesting structures for buildings and farmland.

cases.user_query: What is the stage of groundwater extraction in Thanjavur?

responses.answer: Thanjavur is classified as Safe with a stage of extraction of 58 percent.

cases.user_query: Is groundwater in Nilgiris in good condition?

responses.answer: The Nilgiris is classified as Safe with a stage of extraction of only 21 percent, indicating healthy groundwater conditions.

cases.user_query: What is the extraction stage of Karur district?

responses.answer: Karur district is classified as Critical with a stage of extraction of 96 percent.

cases.user_query: What is AquaMind AI?

responses.answer: AquaMind AI is an AI-powered groundwater intelligence and decision-support system for Tamil Nadu that answers groundwater questions using official datasets and documents.

cases.user_query: What is an aquifer?

responses.answer: An aquifer is an underground layer of water-bearing permeable rock or unconsolidated materials such as gravel, sand, or silt from which groundwater can be extracted.

cases.user_query: What was the rainfall in Vellore during 2023?

responses.answer: The total annual rainfall recorded in Vellore during 2023 was 1024.5 millimetres.

cases.user_query: Will groundwater in Dindigul get worse by 2032?

responses.answer: The current groundwater level in Dindigul is 10.2 metres below ground level.
The predicted groundwater level in Dindigul for 2032 is 13.5 metres below ground level, indicating a decline from the current level.

recommendations.title: Artificial Recharge
recommendations.description: Build percolation ponds to slow the decline in water levels.

recommendations.title: Crop Diversification
recommendations.description: Shift towards less water-intensive crops to reduce extraction.

cases.user_query: What is the groundwater level in Atlantis?

responses.answer: No groundwater records were found for the requested location.

cases.user_query: Show me the groundwater quality index for Cuddalore.

responses.error: The groundwater quality data source was temporarily unavailable.

cases.user_query: How can groundwater be recharged and what do you suggest?

responses.answer: Artificial recharge is the process of augmenting the natural infiltration of surface water into aquifers through engineered structures such as recharge wells, check dams, and percolation tanks.
responses.source: District Recharge Plan of Tamil Nadu

recommendations.title: Check Dams
recommendations.description: Construct check dams across seasonal streams to enhance infiltration.

recommendations.title: Percolation Tanks
recommendations.description: Build percolation tanks in suitable terrain to store and infiltrate runoff.

cases.user_query: Explain the groundwater situation in Namakkal and advise.

responses.answer: Namakkal is classified as Over Exploited with a stage of extraction of 118 percent.
When the stage of extraction exceeds 100 percent, the district is drawing down more groundwater than is recharged each year.
responses.source: Aquifer Management Plan of Namakkal and Salem Districts

recommendations.title: Rainwater Harvesting
recommendations.description: Scale up rainwater harvesting to improve annual recharge.

recommendations.title: Micro-Irrigation
recommendations.description: Adopt drip and sprinkler systems to cut agricultural water use.

cases.user_query: Give me the groundwater status and outlook for Kanyakumari.

responses.answer: Kanyakumari's current groundwater level is 4.2 metres below ground level and its extraction stage is classified as Safe.
The predicted groundwater level in Kanyakumari for 2030 is 4.5 metres below ground level, remaining stable.
A Safe classification indicates that groundwater extraction is well within the sustainable annual recharge.
responses.source: Dynamic Ground Water Resources of Tamil Nadu 2024
